creator: jwb@freebsd.org
text:
Seems to work.  Using the fstab below, booted the head node with compute-001 down (compute node doubles as a file server) and it backgrounded the NFS mount immediately.

FreeBSD head.albacore  bacon ~ 1016: cat /etc/fstab
# Device        Mountpoint      FStype  Options Dump    Pass#
/dev/ada0p2     /               ufs     rw      1       1
/dev/ada0p3     none            swap    sw      0       0
# Added by auto-admin from /usr/local/sbin/auto-add-fdesc-mount
fdesc           /dev/fd                 fdescfs rw              0       0
# End auto-admin addition
# Added by auto-admin from auto-admin
proc                    /proc           procfs  rw              0       0
# End auto-admin addition

compute-001:/compute-001        /compute-001    nfs     rw,bgnow,nfsv4  0    0  

Compute-001 was mounted shortly after coming online:

FreeBSD head.albacore  bacon ~ 1017: df
Filesystem                  Size    Used   Avail Capacity  Mounted on
/dev/ada0p2                 1.8T     15G    1.6T     1%    /
devfs                       1.0K    1.0K      0B   100%    /dev
fdescfs                     1.0K    1.0K      0B   100%    /dev/fd
procfs                      4.0K    4.0K      0B   100%    /proc
map -hosts                    0B      0B      0B   100%    /net
compute-001:/compute-001    7.9T    1.9T    6.0T    24%    /compute-001
